evidence.text: Patient was diagnosed with sepsis due to pneumonia. He was treated with antibiotics and vasopressors. Labs: lactate 1.5 mmol/L, creatinine 1.0 mg/dL. Vital signs: temperature 37.0 C, MAP 80 mmHg.

Labs: lactate 2.5 mmol/L, creatinine 1.2 mg/dL. Patient is suspected to have pneumonia and possible sepsis. Will start antibiotics and monitor vitals and labs closely. Patient's platelet count is 80 10^9/L.

Patient is a 65-year-old male with a history of hypertension and diabetes. He presents with a 2-day history of fever, chills, and cough. Vital signs: temperature 38.5 C, MAP 60 mmHg. Labs: lactate 2.5 mmol/L, creatinine 1.2 mg/dL.
He presents with a 2-day history of fever, chills, and cough.
Patient was diagnosed with sepsis due to pneumonia. He was treated with antibiotics and vasopressors. Labs: lactate 1.5 mmol/L, creatinine 1.0 mg/dL. Vital signs: temperature 37.0 C, MAP 80 mmHg.
He was treated with antibiotics and vasopressors.
Patient is no longer on vasopressors. Nursing: patient tolerating oral intake as ordered. Nursing: skin warm and dry; no new rashes documented. Nursing: pain score reviewed; analgesia as needed.
Vital signs: temperature 37.0 C, MAP 80 mmHg. Patient's condition has improved, and he is being discharged. Patient's platelet count is 100 10^9/L. Patient is no longer on vasopressors.
Patient is no longer on vasopressors.

Patient's mental status has deteriorated, and he is now confused. Will continue to monitor vitals and labs closely and consider changing antibiotics. Patient's platelet count is 70 10^9/L. Patient is still on vasopressors to maintain blood pressure.

Patient's condition has not improved despite antibiotic treatment. Labs: lactate 3.0 mmol/L, creatinine 1.5 mg/dL. Vital signs: temperature 39.0 C, MAP 55 mmHg. Patient's mental status has deteriorated, and he is now confused.

## Determination

## Coding rationale
Clinical validation of billed Sepsis should rest on documented infection plus organ dysfunction indicators (sepsis3_summary, Section: Organ dysfunction indicators (simplified for demo)). Findings that affect payment grouping must be supported by chart indicators (icd10cm_guidelines_fy2026_summary, Section: Sepsis coding notes (high level)). This draft is for auditor review only and is not a payment decision. Key evidence IDs considered: E1, E10, E11, E12, E13, E14, E15, E16, E17, E18, E19, E2, E20, E21, E3, E4, E5, E6, E7, E8, E9.

## Reviewer note
Machine-drafted aid generated from synthetic data. A qualified auditor makes the final determination.
